messages.content:
I can’t run tests from here, because I don’t have access to your environment. However, I can tell you exactly how to run “load test task 7” locally or in CI and how to collect useful results — and I can help interpret output if you paste it.

Short reasoning: I need to know which test runner / load tool and how task 7 is defined so I can give the exact command. Below are common commands and a short checklist you can follow.

Common ways to run a single test/task

- pytest (Python):
  - By name/keyword: pytest -k "task7" -q -s
  - By path: pytest tests/test_load_task7.py::test_task7 -q -s
  - If marked: pytest -m load -q -s

- Jest (Node):
  - npx jest -t "task 7"
  - npm test -- -t "task 7"

- Go:
  - go test ./... -run TestTask7

- Maven (Java):
  - mvn -Dtest=Task7Test test

- Gradle (Java/Kotlin):
  - ./gradlew test --tests "*Task7*"

- Locust (Python load test):
  - locust -f locustfile.py --headless -u 100 -r 10 -t 5m --only-summary
  - To target a particular task in locust, ensure the locustfile exposes a user/task you can select or implement tags and run with --tags.

- k6 (JS load test):
  - k6 run script.js
  - Pass env variable to select scenario: TARGET=task7 k6 run script.js

- Artillery:
  - artillery run load-test.yml -o report.json
  - artillery report report.json

Docker/CI variant (when tests run inside container):
  - Build and run container: docker build -t app-test . && docker run --rm app-test pytest -k "task7"
  - Or docker-compose up -d && docker-compose exec sut pytest -k "task7"

What to check before running
- Required services are running (databases, mock services, backend).
- Environment variables/secrets (API keys, base URL) are set.
- Correct test config (users, concurrency, duration) for a load test.
- Resource limits (don’t overwhelm production endpoints).

How to collect results
- For pytest: use --junitxml=results.xml or pytest-html plugin.
- For load tools: use built-in summary, output to JSON, or generate HTML reports (artillery, k6, locust support these).
- Capture CPU/RAM/latency metrics from target system (Grafana/Prometheus or host monitoring).

If you want, tell me:
- Which tool your project uses for load tests (pytest/locust/k6/artillery/Jest/other),
- How “task 7” is identified (test name, file, scenario name),
- Whether you want a one-off local run or a CI job,
and I’ll give the exact command (and a ready-to-copy script) to run it and collect a useful report.

Paste the test output here after running and I’ll analyze it and suggest fixes or optimizations.
